---
# ── FILL IN YOUR DETAILS ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
title: "Your Post Title"
author: "Your Name"
date: "2025-03-01"
description: "One sentence. Make it good."
categories: [social psychology]
image: "images/cover.jpg"
stream: signal
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

bibliography: references.bib
toc: true

# WebR setup — remove the next two lines if you're not using interactive R
filters:
  - webr
webr:
  packages: ['ggplot2', 'dplyr']
  show-startup-message: false
---

<!-- DELETE THIS BLOCK before submitting ──────────────────────────────────────
STREAM: SIGNAL

This template is for content built to be shared. It should have at least ONE
of: interactive R (WebR), an OJS chart, or an embedded video.

Aim for 600–1000 words of prose, but the interactive elements do a lot of work.
Write with a strong personal voice. You can be funny. You can be direct.

You MUST include at least one of:
  - A WebR interactive code block
  - An Observable JS chart
  - An embedded video

──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── -->


::: {.tldr}
- Your first key point
- Your second key point
- Something the reader can actually do with this
:::


## Your opening

Strong voice. Don't be boring. This stream is for writers who want to be read.

State the thing you're going to show, analyse, or argue — and make it sound worth reading.


## The data / the argument

<!-- ── OPTION A: WebR interactive chart ──────────────────────────────────── -->
<!-- Use this if you want readers to run and edit R code in the browser.       -->

```{webr-r}
#| context: setup
#| autorun: true
# Setup code — hidden from readers, runs first
# Put your data here

set.seed(42)
n <- 100
my_data <- data.frame(
  condition = rep(c("Group A", "Group B"), each = n/2),
  score     = c(rnorm(n/2, mean = 54, sd = 8),
                rnorm(n/2, mean = 61, sd = 9))
)
```

Here's the data. Try changing the numbers and clicking Run to see what happens:

```{webr-r}
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 4
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(my_data, aes(x = condition, y = score, fill = condition)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.75, outlier.shape = NA) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.15, alpha = 0.35, size = 1.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Group A" = "#3d5afe", "Group B" = "#d81b60")) +
  labs(
    title = "Replace with your chart title",
    x = NULL, y = "Score"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
```

<!-- ── OPTION B: Observable JS chart ─────────────────────────────────────── -->
<!-- Use this for slick interactive charts without any R setup.                -->
<!-- Delete the WebR blocks above if you use this instead.                     -->

<!--
```{ojs}
// Your data as a JavaScript array
data = [
  {label: "Group A", value: 54},
  {label: "Group B", value: 61},
  {label: "Group C", value: 49}
]

Plot.plot({
  marks: [
    Plot.barY(data, {x: "label", y: "value", fill: "#3d5afe"}),
    Plot.ruleY([50], {stroke: "#d81b60", strokeDasharray: "4,4"})
  ],
  y: {label: "Score (%)"},
  width: 640,
  height: 320
})
```
-->


## What you make of it

This is the analysis section. What does the data or research actually mean?
Don't just describe — interpret. What would you change if you ran this study?
What question does this raise for you?


## Embedded video (optional but recommended)

<!-- Record 60–90 seconds explaining your main point. Loom is free for students.
     Replace the URL below with your own video link.                           -->

<!-- YouTube:  {{< video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=YOUR_ID >}}           -->
<!-- Vimeo:    {{< video https://vimeo.com/YOUR_ID >}}                         -->
<!-- Loom:     embed the iframe Loom gives you                                 -->
<!-- Local:    {{< video images/my-video.mp4 >}}                               -->


## Takeaway

Short and punchy. What do you want the reader to remember?
One paragraph. Make it land.


---

*Share this on [Bluesky](https://bsky.app/intent/compose?text=PASTE-YOUR-URL-HERE) — or copy the link and send it to someone who'd find it interesting.*

---

::: {.author-card}

<div>
<div class="author-card-name">Your Name</div>
<div class="author-card-bio">
  Psychology student at Goldsmiths, University of London.
  One sentence about your interests.
</div>
</div>
:::

*This post is published under [CC BY 4.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/).*


## References

::: {#refs}
:::
